content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12738305/", "title": "Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in metabolic syndrome: a systematic review and meta-analysis with GRADE evaluation", "summary": "This PRISMA-compliant systematic review and meta-analysis (PROSPERO CRD420251142741) pooled 10 RCTs (n=701 adults, age 25-75, 1-16 week interventions) published 2017-2025 to evaluate intermittent fasting (IF) effects in adults with metabolic syndrome. Interventions included time-restricted eating (TRE), alternate-day fasting (ADF), and intermittent calorie restriction (ICR); comparators were placebo, CER, or usual care. Random-effects pooling showed IF significantly reduced fasting blood sugar (SMD = -0.51; 95% CI: -0.81, -0.20; p = 0.001), insulin (SMD = -0.27; 95% CI: -0.52, -0.03; p = 0.027), HOMA-IR (SMD = -0.39; 95% CI: -0.65, -0.12; p = 0.004), HbA1c (SMD = -0.25; 95% CI: -0.49, -0.02; p = 0.034), LDL-C (SMD = -0.34; 95% CI: -0.53, -0.14; p = 0.001), and IL-6 (SMD = -0.30; 95% CI: -0.57, -0.03; p = 0.029). GRADE rated evidence HIGH quality for FBS, insulin, HOMA-IR, HbA1c, TC, LDL-C, and IL-6. No publication bias detected (Egger and Begg tests p > 0.05). Sensitivity analysis confirmed robustness. Quality was assessed via Cochrane RoB2. Findings support IF as non-pharmacological adjunct for glycemic, lipid, and inflammatory control in MetS adults.", "perspectives": ["Scientific", "Methodological", "Public_Health"], "key_facts": ["10 RCTs, 701 individuals, age range 25-75 years, published 2017-2025, intervention durations 1-16 weeks", "Fasting blood sugar (FBS): SMD = -0.51; 95% CI: -0.81, -0.20; p = 0.001", "HOMA-IR: SMD = -0.39; 95% CI: -0.65, -0.12; p = 0.004; Insulin SMD = -0.27 (-0.52, -0.03), p = 0.027", "LDL-C: SMD = -0.34; 95% CI: -0.53, -0.14; p = 0.001; IL-6 SMD = -0.30 (-0.57, -0.03), p = 0.029", "GRADE rated HIGH quality evidence for FBS, insulin, HOMA-IR, HbA1c, TC, LDL-C and IL-6; PROSPERO registration CRD420251142741"], "knowledge_contribution": "Provides the most comprehensive 2025 meta-analytic quantification of intermittent fasting benefits specifically in metabolic syndrome adults, with GRADE-HIGH ratings on multiple cardiometabolic outcomes, establishing IF as a robust non-pharmacological therapy for glycemic control, LDL-C lowering, and anti-inflammatory effect (IL-6)."}, {"url": "https://www.thelancet.com/journals/eclinm/article/PIIS2589-5370%2824%2900098-1/fulltext?ref=sanatorium.ghost.io", "title": "Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review of systematic reviews and meta-analyses of randomised controlled trials", "summary": "This eClinicalMedicine umbrella review synthesizes 23 prior meta-analyses of RCTs on intermittent fasting (IF) spanning populations with overweight/obesity, type 2 diabetes, metabolic syndrome, NAFLD, and other conditions. Covered IF modalities include ADF, TRE/TRF, TWF (5:2), MADF, IER, with comparators of CER, ad libitum, habitual, and non-intervention diets. Pooled findings across reviews demonstrate consistent body-weight reductions (e.g., Zaman 2023: MD = -2.25 kg; 95% CI: -3.09, -1.42 for TRE), BMI decreases (Cui 2020: MD = -1.20; -1.44, -0.96 for ADF), fat-mass reductions, improvements in triglycerides (Zaman 2023: MD = -18.19 mg/dL for TRE), and glycemic improvements (Gu 2022: HOMA-IR MD = -0.35). Fasting insulin, LDL-C, and blood pressure (DBP up to -5.10 mmHg; Chen 2021) also improved. GRADE quality was mostly Moderate to High, AMSTAR Moderate-High. Important tension: Kim 2022 reported TWF/MADF/TRE INCREASED SBP (SMD = 0.21, 0.05-0.36; p = 0.008) versus CER. Liu 2023 found TRE reduced testosterone (SMD = -0.51; -0.93, -0.10), a potential safety signal for men.", "perspectives": ["Scientific", "Methodological", "Public_Health"], "key_facts": ["Umbrella review covering 23 systematic reviews/meta-analyses of RCTs with populations of overweight/obese, T2DM, MetS, and NAFLD adults; sample sizes up to 2,483 (Gu 2022, 43 studies)", "TRE vs unrestricted diet reduced body weight MD = -2.25 kg (95% CI: -3.09, -1.42; I2 = 93.8%) per Zaman 2023; Liu 2023 TRE MD = -3.08 kg (-5.29, -0.86)", "TRE reduced triglycerides MD = -30.65 mg/dL (95% CI: -52.39, -8.90; p = 0.006) for 7-9h eating windows (Zaman 2023)", "DBP reduction for TRE vs habitual diet MD = -5.10 mmHg (95% CI: -6.27, -3.93; p < 0.001; Chen 2021)", "Safety signals: Kim 2022 reported SBP INCREASED with TWF/MADF/TRE vs CER (SMD = 0.21; 95% CI: 0.05, 0.36; p = 0.008); Liu 2023: TRE reduced testosterone SMD = -0.51 (-0.93, -0.10)"], "knowledge_contribution": "Delivers the highest-level evidence synthesis (umbrella review across 23 meta-analyses) quantifying effect sizes for weight, body composition, lipids, glycemia, and blood pressure with GRADE ratings, while uniquely highlighting safety tensions (SBP elevation vs CER, testosterone reduction in men) that are rarely surfaced in primary IF benefit narratives."}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0271531725000600", "title": "The metabolic effects of intermittent fasting in patients with type 2 diabetes", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: paywalled/shell", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": "No content retrievable; ScienceDirect returned a captcha/robot-check shell page (timestamp 2026-04-15 17:50:17 UTC, reference 9ecccab56d11fc1b). No substantive information could be extracted."}, {"url": "https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s12937-025-01178-6", "title": "Effects of intermittent fasting on body composition and cardiometabolic health in overweight and obese adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials", "summary": "This PRISMA-compliant systematic review and meta-analysis (PROSPERO CRD420251036588) pooled 15 RCTs (n=758 overweight/obese adults with BMI >= 25) searching PubMed, Embase, and Web of Science through March 2025, comparing IF (IER, ADF, 5:2, TRF/TRE) against continuous energy restriction (CER) or ad libitum control. Risk-of-bias used Cochrane RoB1 via RevMan 5.4. Random-effects pooling demonstrated IF significantly reduced body weight (MD = -3.73 kg; 95% CI: -5.29, -2.17) and BMI (MD = -1.04 kg/m^2; 95% CI: -1.39, -0.70). Lipid improvements included total cholesterol (MD = -6.31 mg/dL; 95% CI: -12.36, -0.26) and LDL (MD = -5.44 mg/dL; 95% CI: -12.36, -0.26). DBP decreased (MD = -3.30 mmHg; 95% CI: -5.47, -1.13); SBP, FPG, and HbA1c showed no significant change. Short-term IF (<=12 weeks) caused a TEMPORARY rise in triglycerides (MD = +13.22 mg/dL; 95% CI: 3.39, 23.05), while long-term (>12 weeks) optimized lipid benefits. Subgroup analysis: ADF was superior to TRE for weight loss and LDL improvement. Effects are time-dependent; personalized protocols recommended.", "perspectives": ["Scientific", "Methodological", "Public_Health"], "key_facts": ["15 RCTs, n = 758 overweight/obese adults (BMI >= 25); search through March 2025; PROSPERO CRD420251036588", "Body weight MD = -3.73 kg (95% CI: -5.29, -2.17); BMI MD = -1.04 kg/m^2 (95% CI: -1.39, -0.70)", "LDL MD = -5.44 mg/dL (95% CI: -12.36, -0.26); TC MD = -6.31 mg/dL (95% CI: -12.36, -0.26); DBP MD = -3.30 mmHg (95% CI: -5.47, -1.13)", "Short-term IF (<=12 weeks) TEMPORARY triglyceride elevation MD = +13.22 mg/dL (95% CI: 3.39, 23.05); no significant effect on SBP, FPG, or HbA1c", "Subgroup analysis: alternate-day fasting (ADF) superior to time-restricted eating (TRE) for weight loss and LDL improvement; 1.77 billion overweight / 1.53 billion obese adults projected by 2035 (World Obesity Atlas 2024)"], "knowledge_contribution": "Introduces a time-dependent model of IF effects: short-term (<=12 weeks) may transiently WORSEN triglycerides while long-term (>12 weeks) optimizes lipid metabolism. Head-to-head modality ranking (ADF > TRE) for weight loss and LDL improvement differentiates it from prior umbrella reviews that treated IF as monolithic."}, {"url": "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40826125/", "title": "The effect of intermittent fasting on insulin resistance, lipid profile, and inflammation on metabolic syndrome: a GRADE assessed systematic review and meta-analysis", "summary": "This PubMed-indexed systematic review and meta-analysis (PMID 40826125, PMCID PMC12363089, DOI 10.1186/s41043-025-01039-2) searched PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science through March 2025 for RCTs on fasting interventions in adults with metabolic syndrome, applying GRADE evidence assessment and Cochrane RoB2 quality grading. Eight RCTs with 573 participants were pooled. Fasting significantly reduced fasting blood glucose (WMD = -3.34 mg/dL; 95% CI: -6.24, -0.45; P = 0.024), HbA1c (WMD = -0.08; 95% CI: -0.13, -0.02; P = 0.005), and HOMA-IR (WMD = -0.60; 95% CI: -0.91, -0.28; P < 0.05). Lipid improvements (LDL-C) and inflammatory reduction (IL-6) were also demonstrated. The abstract acknowledges prior controversial evidence and positions this GRADE-assessed synthesis as reconciliation. Authors concluded fasting improved glycemic control (FBS, HbA1c, HOMA-IR), lipid metabolism (LDL-C), and inflammatory state (IL-6) in adults with metabolic syndrome. References note a companion Front Nutr 2025 paper (PMID 41459076) and a 2026 immunosenescence review (Front Nutr 2026;13:1736969; PMID 41646173) linking IF to immune aging.", "perspectives": ["Scientific", "Methodological", "Public_Health"], "key_facts": ["8 RCTs, 573 participants, search through March 2025; PMID 40826125, PMCID PMC12363089, DOI 10.1186/s41043-025-01039-2", "Fasting blood glucose: WMD = -3.34 mg/dL (95% CI: -6.24, -0.45; P = 0.024)", "HbA1c: WMD = -0.08 (95% CI: -0.13, -0.02; P = 0.005)", "HOMA-IR: WMD = -0.60 (95% CI: -0.91, -0.28; P < 0.05)", "Methodology: GRADE approach for evidence quality, Cochrane RoB2 for bias; databases searched PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, Web of Science"], "knowledge_contribution": "Provides a compact GRADE-assessed quantification of IF benefits on insulin resistance (HOMA-IR), glycemic control (FBS, HbA1c), LDL-C and IL-6 in an 8-RCT MetS cohort, using WMD effect sizes (vs SMD) to corroborate the PMC12738305 meta-analysis and strengthen confidence in glycemic-control plus anti-inflammatory claims."}, {"url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/398583827_Intermittent_fasting_improves_metabolic_outcomes_in_metabolic_syndrome_a_systematic_review_and_meta-analysis_with_GRADE_evaluation", "title": "Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in metabolic syndrome: a systematic review and meta-analysis with GRADE evaluation (ResearchGate)", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: paywalled/shell", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": "No content retrievable; ResearchGate returned a Cloudflare security-check shell (Ray ID 9ecccaaf59793cf6). Page title corresponds to the same study available at PMC12738305, so substantive extraction was obtained from that source."}]}
